title: Populating Next Right Pointers in Each Node II
hints: Consider processing the tree level by level. How can you traverse all nodes at the current depth to link them, then move to the next depth?
If you cannot use a queue for BFS, think about using the `next` pointers of the current level to traverse and link the nodes of the next level.
When linking children of the current level, handle gaps between siblings by connecting a node's right child to the leftmost child of its `next` neighbor's subtree.
